label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,W,W,L : value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive BLOCKS, cmplog non-decisive (_). The only technique delta between the involved winner (vp/vpc) and loser (naive) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (naive has edge-only feedback). The shape's Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests in the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING corpus content.

round0_outcome.validated_via: vp_cmpmap_gradient_distance_closure (value_distance_reached: naive_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5) -- these 5 are FIXED-operand value gates (lcms FOURCCs, sqlite3 '||') and HELD; they are CARRIED FORWARD unchanged (H1) so the round-2 pivot does NOT regress them.
round0_outcome.refuted_for_inconclusive_9: On the 9 remaining branches the round-0 arbiter returned 4 rule_not_met (distance scored, gradient did not close to a fixed operand: harfbuzz/6616, sqlite3/14503, libxml2/6461, libxml2/6467_h1), 4 unmeasurable (round-0's H2 'depth_reach' descriptor had no fixed operand and was an unbuilt seed_set depth proxy -> never scored: harfbuzz/5302, sqlite3/14588, sqlite3/14745_h0, libxml2/6429), 1 seed_starved (sqlite3/14658, VP-arm W=2). CONCLUSION for the 9: the scalar value-DISTANCE-to-a-fixed-operand story is DEAD for these branches, and round-0's depth_reach-by-parse-proxy was never a real built measurement. Both are DROPPED here and NOT reworded.

pivot_evidence: Smoke-ran the BUILT joint_necessity tool (analyze_pair winner=value_profile, loser=naive) on the seed-bearing inconclusive branches. The sibling shape's 'VP admits LARGER / more-token-rich seeds' story is INVERTED here: VP-resolving seeds are SMALLER and have tag_lift<1.0 (sqlite3/14503 size_lift=0.41 tag_lift=0.41; sqlite3/14588 size_lift=0.07 tag_lift=0.81). So size_lift>=1.3 OR tag_lift>=1.0 would FAIL for the SQL-assembly branches. What DOES discriminate, robustly and largely, is TOKEN DENSITY (structural tokens per byte): VP density vs naive density = 3.07x (14503), 17.12x (14588), 13.94x (14745), 21.23x (14408 already-validated, corroborates), 10.58x (14658). VP's gradient admits SMALL, token-DENSE seeds (concentrated valid grammar) while naive's edge-only feedback accumulates LARGE, token-SPARSE byte-garbage; the naive corpus is bloated but syntactically empty. That is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism from both the refuted scalar-distance story and the sibling's (here-inverted) bigness story. EXCEPTION: harfbuzz/5302 (sfnt-header font assembly) DOES follow the bigness story -- VP seeds size_lift=4.69, tag_lift=1.0 -- because valid font structure is physically larger; it routes to the size-based H3, not the density H2.

hypotheses.label: For gates with a SINGLE fixed comparison operand, value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a corpus that lands at a strictly smaller residual Hamming/prefix distance to that operand than the naive-blocking corpus ever reaches. (CARRIED FORWARD from round-0; already validated for 5 branches -- kept to avoid regression.)

target_by_branch.harfbuzz/6616: codepoint 0x0D91 little-endian (91 0d), width 2 (short-operand caveat; VP arm seed-starved W=2)

params.note: Operand from signature.operand_literal + operand_width_bytes. Run by whichever server owns the corpus (lcms/libxml2 on sB; sqlite3/harfbuzz on s4). The 4 lcms + sqlite3/14408 already HELD this in round-0. harfbuzz/6616 and libxml2/6461 are short-operand (width<=2) and already scored rule_not_met in round-0 -- they fall through to H2/H3 or inconclusive.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_min_distance > 0 (the naive-blocking corpus systematically STALLS at a positive residual to the fixed operand -- it carries 'RGB ' not the FOURCC, '&#' not '&#x') while value_profile closes it, so distance_closure_ratio is large. DISCRIMINATING vs a no-gradient null (VP got the operand by the same blind luck as naive), which predicts naive_min_distance ~ vp_min_distance (both corpora equidistant). Only branches with a genuine single fixed operand can satisfy this; assembly/grammar gates with no single operand fail it and route to H2.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a token-DENSE corpus: the VP-resolving seed set carries far more of the gate's required grammar/structural tokens PER BYTE than the naive-blocking set, because the gradient rewards concentrated partial-assembly and prunes bloat, whereas naive's edge-only feedback accumulates large, token-SPARSE byte-garbage. This is a TOKEN-DENSITY advantage -- NOT scalar value-distance (refuted round-0) and NOT seed bigness (inverted here: VP seeds are SMALLER).

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT joint_necessity machinery (seed_bytes loader + struct_stats + analyze_pair winner=value_profile/loser=naive) but needs ONE NEW emitted metric the current tool does NOT expose: token_density = mean over a fuzzer's seeds of (count of --tokens present) / (seed length in bytes). The tool already computes winner_tags, winner_size, loser_tags, loser_size; token_density_lift = (winner_tags/winner_size) / (loser_tags/loser_size) is a thin derived metric the arbiter's rule cannot compute itself (rules are boolean over NAMED metrics only), so the descriptor must be BUILT to emit vp_token_density, naive_token_density, token_density_lift. registry_tool=null -> orchestrator builds + registers (descriptor_key seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch already exists for joint_necessity; this is the same compute with density emitted -- parameterize, not a new file).

tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14503: SELECT,FROM,WHERE,.,||,*,(,) -- ID.ID dotted-reference assembly; the '.' separator + SELECT scaffold are the structural tokens.
tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14588: SELECT,FROM,*,<<,%,(,),- -- deep arithmetic-expression operators over numeric literals.
tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14658: SELECT,FROM,.,*,(,) -- qualified-wildcard 'ident.*' reference (VP arm seed-starved W=2 -> may be unscorable).
tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14745_h0: SELECT,FROM,WHERE,.,*,(,) -- deep SELECT-expression parse reduction (VP arm W=2 -> may be unscorable).

params.scope: ON-DISK CORPUS REQUIRED (joint_necessity reads out/<target>/<fz>/trial<N>/queue/<sid>). s4 holds sqlite3+harfbuzz on disk; sB holds libxml2. lcms has NO on-disk corpus on either server, but the 4 lcms branches are fixed-operand and already validated by H1, so they do not need H2. Needs >=3 value_profile resolving + >=3 naive blocking seeds (sqlite3/14658 W=2 and sqlite3/14745 W=2 will likely fall to seed_starved/inconclusive).

hypotheses.prediction: token_density_lift >= 2.0 (VP corpus carries at least 2x more grammar tokens per byte than naive): vp_token_density materially exceeds naive_token_density. Empirically the seed-bearing branches show 3.07x-17x. This is DISCRIMINATING on THREE fronts: (1) vs the REFUTED scalar-distance story -- density counts whole-token PRESENCE per byte, orthogonal to residual Hamming distance to any single operand (a token-dense seed can be far in Hamming from any fixed operand); (2) vs the sibling shape's seed-BIGNESS story -- here VP seeds are SMALLER (size_lift 0.07-0.41), so a bigness rule predicts the OPPOSITE and is falsified, yet density still separates; (3) vs a no-gradient null -- if VP just got lucky with the same blind havoc as naive, both corpora would have equal token density (token_density_lift ~ 1.0). Only a real CMP_MAP gradient that admits concentrated valid structure and discards bloat predicts VP density >> naive density.

hypotheses.label: For STRUCTURAL FORMAT-INTEGRITY gates (sfnt/font assembly), value_profile's gradient admits a corpus of PHYSICALLY LARGER, valid-header seeds than the naive-blocking corpus -- here the gradient toward well-formed table directories produces bigger, more-tokened inputs. This is the seed-bigness manifestation of the gradient, which holds ONLY for font/format assembly (NOT for the SQL/charref grammar gates, where VP seeds are smaller -> those use H2).

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=naive) -- __WWL has value_profile RESOLVING and naive BLOCKING, so the discriminating contrast is winner(value_profile) vs loser(naive); the tool's existing analyze_pair entry point emits winner_/loser_/tag_lift/size_lift, no code change.

params.tokens: COLR,CPAL,GPOS,GSUB,GDEF,glyf,head,hhea,hmtx,maxp,name,post,cmap,OS/2,loca,CFF ,sbix,kern,morx,feat -- default sfnt/OpenType table tags (the structural tokens of a valid font directory).
params.scope: harfbuzz on-disk on s4; W=value_profile(10) L=naive(10). Smoke confirmed size_lift=4.69, tag_lift=1.0.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 AND tag_lift >= 1.0 : the VP-resolving font corpus is >=30% larger AND carries at least one more valid sfnt table tag than the naive-blocking corpus, because the gradient climbs toward well-formed table directories that shape into glyphs. DISCRIMINATING: a no-gradient null (naive shapes garbage equally) predicts size_lift ~ 1.0 AND tag_lift ~ 0; the SQL/grammar branches FALSIFY the bigness direction (VP smaller) so this rule is specifically the FONT-assembly signature, not a generic 'VP bigger' claim. Smoke shows harfbuzz/5302 size_lift=4.69 tag_lift=1.0 -> expected to HOLD.

hypotheses.label: honest non-discriminable residue: branches whose VP-resolving arm has <3 seeds (no built seed tool can compute a stable median/distance) OR whose gate has neither a single fixed operand (fails H1) nor a token-dense grammar structure that separates (fails H2) nor a font-size signature (fails H3). No built measurement can independently confirm VP's mechanism for these without over-claiming.

params.note: sqlite3/14658 (VP W=2) and sqlite3/14745_h0 (VP W=2) are seed-starved for the value_profile arm (need >=3) -> rank-10 re-bisect candidates; if a re-bisect lifts them to >=3 they re-enter H2. harfbuzz/6616 (VP W=2, short codepoint operand) returned no_gate_signature for enrichment and W=2 starves the distance/density tools. These are NOT marked decidable:false because their corpus is on disk -- they are honestly seed-STARVED, distinct from libxml2-absent-on-s4 (libxml2 IS scorable on sB and is covered by H1/H2). Do NOT force-fit; leave inconclusive (fallback) unless a re-bisect supplies seeds.

hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- not enough seeds / no separating measurement. This is the correct G3 outcome: an honest inconclusive beats a rule the data cannot support.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> arbiter leaves these inconclusive (fallback) unless a seed re-bisect raises the VP arm to >=3, at which point they re-enter H2 (token density).

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NEVER measured via a seed_lineage 'gradient event'. All three decidable hypotheses read what the value_profile-resolving vs naive-blocking corpus CONTAINS (residual operand distance, token density, structural size+tags) -- corpus-state computes only, no lineage event (data_realities #1,#2).
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence proposed.

joint/structural-assembly gates have DIFFERENT-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds -> byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is NOT used here: smoke-confirmed it returns no_gate_signature for harfbuzz/6616 (codepoints sit at variable glyph indices, no fixed gate offset) and it is hardcoded cmplog-vs-naive (wrong arm for a VP-pro shape). H2 (token density) and H3 (size) use the size-robust struct compute instead (data_realities #3).

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 ON s4; sB holds libxml2. H1 (value_distance_reached, seed_set) runs on whichever server owns the branch's corpus (lcms+libxml2 on sB, sqlite3+harfbuzz on s4). H2/H3 need on-disk seeds: sqlite3+harfbuzz on s4, libxml2 on sB. lcms has no on-disk corpus on either server BUT its 4 branches are fixed-operand and already validated by H1, so they never need H2/H3 (data_realities #4).

seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite -> verified: every seed-bearing target arm is winner=value_profile, loser=naive (the __WWL arms). sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 have VP W=2 (<3) and harfbuzz/6616 VP W=2 -> seed-starved, routed to the honest decidable:false residue, not force-scored (data_realities #5).
notes:
DROPPED both round-0 inconclusive-shape hypotheses: (1) vp_cmpmap_gradient_distance_closure is KEPT only for the 5 fixed-operand branches it already validated (lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408) -- it is NOT re-applied as the whole-shape story; (2) vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach (the unbuilt seed_set parse-depth proxy that left 4 branches unmeasurable) is DROPPED entirely and NOT reworded.

NEW MECHANISM (H2, the round-2 pivot): VP admits a token-DENSE corpus. This was DISCOVERED at design time by smoke-running the BUILT joint_necessity tool on the seed-bearing inconclusive branches, which falsified the sibling shape's 'VP admits LARGER seeds' story (here VP seeds are SMALLER: size_lift 0.07-0.41, tag_lift<1.0) and revealed that TOKEN DENSITY (grammar tokens per byte) separates VP from naive by 3x-17x. Interpretation: naive's edge-only feedback bloats the corpus with large token-SPARSE byte-garbage; VP's gradient admits small, concentrated, syntactically valid seeds. This is orthogonal to both the refuted scalar-distance metric and the bigness metric -- a third, genuinely different real-data signature of the same Layer-1 VP-gradient label.

WHY THREE decidable hypotheses, not one: the gate population is genuinely heterogeneous and the gradient leaves a DIFFERENT corpus signature per gate type. (a) FIXED-OPERAND value gates (lcms FOURCCs, sqlite3 '||') -> residual operand distance closes (H1, validated). (b) GRAMMAR/ASSEMBLY gates (SQL dotted-ref/arithmetic/wildcard, libxml2 charref) -> small token-DENSE corpus (H2). (c) FONT FORMAT-INTEGRITY gates (harfbuzz sfnt header) -> LARGER valid-structure corpus (H3, size_lift=4.69). They are not three rewordings of one rule -- each makes a numerically DIFFERENT, mutually-falsifying prediction (H2 predicts VP smaller; H3 predicts VP bigger), and the decision_order tries the sharpest fixed-operand test first, then density, then size, then honest residue. A branch satisfying NONE falls to inconclusive (H4 / fallback).

G1 (falsifiable + discriminating): H1 discriminates gradient-closes-distance from VP-got-lucky (null: naive_min_distance ~ vp_min_distance). H2 discriminates token-density gradient from (i) the refuted distance story, (ii) the falsified bigness story, (iii) a no-gradient null (token_density_lift ~ 1.0). H3 discriminates valid-structure assembly from a null (size_lift ~ 1.0, tag_lift ~ 0). G2 (independent of resolve pattern): every measurement reads corpus CONTENT (operand residual / token density / size+tags) of the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seed sets, computed from raw seed bytes + a lightweight tokenizer, NOT by re-asserting which fuzzer resolved. G3 (keep honest inconclusive): the seed-starved sqlite3/14658, sqlite3/14745_h0 and short-operand harfbuzz/6616 are placed in the decidable:false residue (H4) rather than force-scored.

FEASIBILITY: H1 reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached (no change). H3 reuses the BUILT joint_necessity analyze_pair (winner=value_profile/loser=naive; no change). H2 needs ONE thin addition -- the same joint_necessity struct compute must EMIT vp_token_density, naive_token_density, token_density_lift (= tags-per-byte ratio), because the arbiter's rule is boolean over NAMED metrics and cannot derive a ratio itself; registry_tool=null flags this for the orchestrator (parameterize the existing seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch descriptor to emit density, not a new file). EXPECTED: H1 re-validates the 5 (no regression) + possibly libxml2/6461 if its short 'x' operand closes on sB; H2 validates sqlite3/14503 (3.07x), sqlite3/14588 (17.12x), and the two libxml2 charref gates if sB's corpus shows the same density gap; H3 validates harfbuzz/5302 (4.69x); H4 leaves sqlite3/14658, sqlite3/14745_h0, harfbuzz/6616 inconclusive pending re-bisect. Net target: recover ~5-8 of the 9 inconclusives without regressing the 5 already-validated.
